video_title: Top 30 #DDD Videos of ALL TIME with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Dr. Feelgood's kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: It's the beautiful high desert of Santa Fe, New Mexico. So when I hear there's a chef that's all about farm to table, and he's got a restaurant where the name kind of plays a little homage to one of my favorites, Motley Crue, you know I got to check it out. This is Dr. Feelgood's kitchen. All put together by Josh Gerwin, who's blazing his own trail as a Josh of all trades.

dishes.description: Bacon belly cured for 7-10 days with brown sugar, black pepper, pink salt, and kosher salt, then applewood smoked for 4-6 hours. Ground into a 9oz patty with lean meat, seasoned with kosher salt and white pepper, and cooked in a wood-fired oven. Served on house-made potato bread (kaiser roll) with mayonnaise, heirloom tomatoes, and organic greens.
dishes.guy_response: Bro, this is what everybody wants a BLT to be like. You're getting the volume of a burger, the flavor of the bacon, the whole top of that patty is just all that crispy, crunchy, unctuous flavor. Then on the inside, it's super tender. It's just done perfectly. I mean, it's BLT of another level.

restaurants.name: Monument Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: So here we are in Georgetown, about 30 miles from Boston, to check out Monument Cafe. And he's been mixing farm fresh flavors with a taste of Texas since opening this joint in 95.

dishes.description: A Texas staple made with oil, onions, bell pepper, celery, garlic, sliced mushrooms, deglazed with white wine, seasoned with salt, black pepper, cumin (comino), chili powder, Rotel tomatoes and green chilies. A roux is built with flour and chicken stock, finished with whole milk. Layered with tortillas, pulled dark and white chicken, Monterey Jack and cheddar cheese, and sauce. Baked for 45 minutes at 350 degrees. Served with pinto beans and roasted root vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: So it's green chilies. They come through creamy, not spicy, which gets misinterpreted a lot of times when you talk about Tex-Mex food. Just like Matthew was saying, having that dark meat, I think you did the right balance. Yeah, real good consistency, man. Not too wet, not too dry. Shuffle down a plateful of that? Easily. Cachelata. That's like Texas comfort food.

restaurants.name: Super Duper Weenie
restaurants.guy_intro: Now, I've been hearing a lot about a place called Super Duper Weenie in Connecticut. I'm telling you, I did some great field testing, and that's what brings us here to Fairfield, Connecticut, to the Super Duper Weenie World Headquarters. Because Gary Zimola is serious about his dogs.

dishes.description: Hot dog sliced and cooked on the grill until crispy. Topped with bacon, sauerkraut, spicy brown mustard, house-made sweet cucumber relish (made with chopped cucumbers, red and green bell peppers, onions, mustard seed, celery seed, sugar, cider vinegar, water, simmered and blended for texture), and chopped onions.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's good. The hidden surprise in all that is the bacon. Just a little bit of crunch. It just fortifies it. And the sweetness of the relish just takes it all. It's the amalgamator. Where's that been all my life? Never tasted anything like the relish. Best hot dog ever.

restaurants.name: Desert Oak Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in El Paso, Texas. I'm in a strip mall. And I'm looking for, wait for it, barbecue. I mean, El Paso, Texas, real deal. Barbecue, strip mall. Don't be kidding me until I heard about this dude. He's got a big monster smoker that's a stick burner. I mean, no gas assist. It's real deal. This is Desert Oak Barbecue.

dishes.dish_name: El Paso Stuffed Potato with Brisket
dishes.description: A large baked potato, wrapped in foil and smoked for 4 hours. Stuffed with butter, salt, pepper, cheese, sour cream, chives, a quarter-pound of 18-hour oak-smoked brisket (trimmed, seasoned with salt and pepper), pinto beans, and jalapenos toreados (roasted jalapenos marinated overnight in lime juice, soy sauce, and onions).
dishes.guy_response: Wow. Toriato is one of my new favorite things. One thing I like on your brisket, it's not overly smoky, nicely seasoned, good bark, beans are delicious. The whole thing is a package deal, man, besides the fact that it's not a human size. It's like four human sizes. Excellent.

restaurants.name: Sonny's Famous Steak Hoagies
restaurants.guy_intro: We got to check out Sonny's famous steak hoagies.

dishes.description: Made with thinly sliced boneless ribeye (1/16th of an inch), cooked on the griddle with yellow onions and butter. Mixed together and topped with provolone cheese. Served on freshly baked house-made hoagie rolls (potato bread dough, mixed for 12 mins, proofed for an hour, portioned, rolled into kaiser shape, proofed, baked at 350 for 20 mins).
dishes.guy_response: You know what the kicker on that is? The butter. The butter is what does that.

restaurants.name: The Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in South New Jersey in a town called Summers Point. Now, this is where people cruise through to go down to the boardwalk in Ocean City. But more importantly, to visit this joint. This is the Grilled Cheese and Crab Cake Company.

dishes.description: A grilled cheese sandwich with a crab cake in the middle. Crab cake made with jumbo lump crab meat, mayo, eggs, fresh lemon juice, honey mustard, sriracha, crystal hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, Old Bay seasoning, celery seed, baking powder, parsley, and panko breadcrumbs. Crab cake is cooked on buttered grill, bread is buttered, Monterey Jack cheese is added, and steamed to melt cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Crab cake is a little bit creamier than most people expect. Crispy on the outside, tons of crab. No question about the amount of crab in there. Nice crunch of the toast. Not too much cheese. You pull it off, man. Nicely done, man. Thank you. Nicely done.

restaurants.name: Krakatoa
restaurants.guy_intro: This is Krakatoa.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced sirloin cooked with red seasoning (bumbu merah: chili de arbol, guajillo, galangal, ginger, candlenut, shallot, garlic, lemongrass, nutmeg, cumin, coriander, turmeric, cameron paste, lime leaf, water, salt, oil), coconut oil, cardamom, cloves, star anise, cinnamon, chicken base, coconut milk, red pepper, onion, lime leaf, lemongrass, and Indonesian sweet soy sauce (ketchup). Served with rice and sambal (red chili, lemongrass, lime leaf, oil, water).
dishes.guy_response: Very Thai-esque. So much lemongrass. And the coconut milk. Love the fresh veggies in there. The spice is great. Rustic. Flavorful. It's not every bite is the same. Kind of like listening to a song, and all of a sudden you get a lot of bass. And you get a lot of drum. And you get a lot of treble. I'm in treble right now because that sambal is hot. It's juicy. And you get to taste every nuance of the clove, the star anise, the cinnamon. It's just so good.

restaurants.name: Saffron Street
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm back here in Calgary, Alberta, on First Street, at First Street Market Big Food Hall. And you know what? We're talking about Indian food. Not Northern Indian, which everybody really knows. I'm talking Southern Indian, the street food, the handheld food. I hear there's two buddies that are in here, and they're knocking it out, and they're scratch-making it all. This is Saffron Street.

dishes.description: A crepe (dosa) made from a fermented batter of sona masuri rice and black lentils (urad dal) with fenugreek seeds, ground in a wet stone grinder, then fermented for 12 hours. Filled with masala (hot pan, canola oil, mustard seeds, green chilies, red chilies, curry leaves, onions, ginger garlic paste, turmeric, mashed potatoes, salt, cilantro). Served with coconut chutney (desiccated coconut, green chilies, cumin seeds, ginger garlic paste, salt, roasted chana dal, water, tempered with mustard seeds, urad dal, red chili, curry leaf) and sambar (oil, mustard seeds, green chilies, coriander seeds, red chili, curry leaf, chopped onions, ginger garlic paste, coriander powder, kashmiri, water, tomatoes, yellow lentils, salt, sugar).
dishes.guy_response: There are very few things in this world that have that much flavor, that much texture. That's outstanding. The coconut, tangy, subtle, delicious. The sambar is a little bit sweet. The tomato chutney is more like a salsa. And then you get the meatiness and the body from the potato. That's in the top five best Indian dishes I've ever had.

restaurants.name: Crazy Jim's Limpy Burger
restaurants.guy_intro: So when I'm cruising the quad at the University of Michigan in my MAX 67 Camaro, a question comes to mind. Where should I eat? Now, if you ask anybody in the student body where to go for the bomb burger, they're going to say Crazy Jim's Limpy Burger.

dishes.dish_name: Limpy Burger (Double with Hot Peppers, Onions, Pumpernickel)
dishes.description: Fresh ground beef, double-ground, never frozen, made into 1.6 oz patties. Cooked on the griddle. Guy orders a double with hot peppers, onions, and pumpernickel bun. The restaurant offers various sizes (singles to stack of five, or custom sizes like 10 on kaiser) and almost 30 toppings, cheeses, and buns.
dishes.guy_response: That meat is so tender. Wow. That's tasty. The cheddar, the peppers. The beauty is layering and you get all your flavors mingling just the right way.

restaurants.name: The Pig Witch
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Kansas City, Missouri. You do not roll into this town without calling the head coach of the Kansas City Chiefs, Coach Reed, and saying, listen, buddy, where are we going to eat? We're going to a butcher shop that's also the greatest sandwich shop you've ever eaten at. The real deal? The real deal. What's it called? The Pig Witch.

dishes.description: A burger patty made from smoked pork butt (rubbed with house barbecue rub, smoked with hickory wood for 5 hours at 250F, then ground with kidney die for big chunks), ground raw pork, more barbecue rub, minced garlic, and ground bacon trimmings. Served on a bun with ranch dressing, andouille spread (spicy spreadable salami), house barbecue sauce, B&B (bread and butter) jalapenos, pepper jack, cheddar, and provolone cheeses.
dishes.guy_response: Wow. I love the crisp on the pork, and I'm loving the patty. Three cheeses, man, blend perfect. How about the jalapenos? Man alive. Bread and butter jalapenos is my new favorite thing. The ranch dressing, you need that cooling, creaming effect. My favorite part of the whole thing, though, is the pulled pork patty. My favorite part.

restaurants.name: Kansas City Taco Company
restaurants.guy_intro: Now, I'm here to check out this place where a dude from Newport, Rhode Island ended up coming to Kansas City to open up a taco place and do tacos his way. This is Kansas City Taco Company.

dishes.description: Flour tortilla filled with mesquite-smoked pork (smoked for an hour, then wet-rubbed with chipotle in adobo, oregano, black pepper, garlic powder, salt, olive oil, red bell pepper, julienne onions, cold water, then oven-cooked overnight), chorizo (ground picnic pork with oregano, cinnamon, cloves, cayenne pepper, coriander, cumin, salt, pepper, sugar, garlic powder, chili powder, paprika, pureed chipotle in adobo, apple cider vinegar), fried, and served on a bed of Spanish rice. Topped with queso blanco (red onions, milk, processed cheese, cream, charred serrano peppers), spicy bacon pinto beans, fresh pico de gallo, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: You know what that's lacking? No. Nothing. Brother, you are the flavor man. It's balanced, delicious heat. The beans are definitely a plus. You've got smoke in the pork. The queso, it's creamy silky. Love the pico on there. That's a really good burrito.

restaurants.name: Saboris International Cuisine
restaurants.guy_intro: This is Saboris International Cuisine.

dishes.description: Hamburger with cheese, bacon, peanut butter, chipotle aioli (chipotle peppers, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper, chipotle powder, mayonnaise), sriracha sauce, tomatoes, and onions. Served with house chips (purple yam, regular yam, potatoes, Japanese yam, taro root, lotus root, fried and seasoned with garlic powder, cumin, achiote, paprika, onion powder, salt, pepper, white pepper).
dishes.guy_response: That's good. That chipotle aioli and a little bit of the sriracha take the burger from being sweet and really heavy peanut butter to being a little spicy peanut butter, like a Thai peanut dipping sauce. Delicious chips. I see what everybody liked about the peanut butter burger. Maybe it just took me some time.

restaurants.name: Tandoori Oven
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Logan, Utah, about an hour and a half outside of Salt Lake City. But these days, I'm looking for Indian food. Oh, you're seeing it sprout up all over the country. Big flavors, great stories. And I got one for you right here at a gas station. This is Tandoori Oven.

dishes.description: Chicken marinated for 48 hours in homemade yogurt, salt, paprika, turmeric, garam masala, lemon juice, ginger garlic, and food coloring, then cooked in a tandoor oven at 400F with mesquite charcoal. Sauce made from onion gravy (oil, cumin, coriander, onions, tomato, water) and a separate sauce (oil, ginger garlic, garam masala, turmeric, paprika, added to onion/tomato base, blended). Chicken is added to sauce with ginger garlic, bell pepper, cream, and simmered until boiled. Served with rice (cloves, bay leaf, cumin seed) and garnished with fenugreek leaf and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, so good. This is very approachable. That's probably one of the staple dishes that people should try when they're trying Indian food for the first time. Oh, my gosh. I'm telling you. Ah. Mm. Thank you.

restaurants.name: Bagelsaurus
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Cambridge Summerville area right outside of Boston to check out a joint where this chef was going to be a schoolteacher. Once you kind of change gears and decided to open a restaurant offering up one of my favorite things, it's something that takes so much time, so much energy, and it's really a labor of love. It's bagels. This is Bagelsaurus.

dishes.dish_name: Onion Bagel BLT with Spicy Pepper Cream Cheese
dishes.description: House-made onion bagel (sourdough starter, malt syrup, flour, table salt, instant yeast, baked onions, coffee seeds, mixed for 9 mins, proofed for hours with folds, cooled overnight, boiled for 30-40 secs, baked for 20-50 mins). Toasted and spread with spicy pepper cream cheese (house-pickled jalapenos, roasted poblanos, red chili flake, salt, cream cheese). Topped with oven-roasted tomatoes (plum tomatoes, chopped garlic, salt, sugar, pepper, thyme, extra virgin olive oil, roasted for 4 hours at 250F), red leaf lettuce, and bacon.
dishes.guy_response: The only thing that I don't like about this bagel is that one quarter of it's already gone. As a bagel, it's of the best we've ever had on the show. Light, tender, great chew. Bacon's great. But these oven roasted plum tomatoes, the acid of that cut with the fat and the spiciness of the jalapenos and poblanos in the cream cheese, you're a dangerous woman. I'm not kidding you.

restaurants.name: Full Belly Deli
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm just like you guys. I'm always looking for great restaurants. I'm always on the hunt. So a few years ago, I was in Truckee, California. Everybody tells you about this place. You got to get the sandwich here. So I did. And it was dynamite. Well, now I'm in Reno, Nevada. And I find out these dudes have got a second location. I got to check it out. This is Full Belly Deli.

dishes.description: Flour tortilla filled with hash browns, sliced house-made biscuits (organic AP flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, cold butter, buttermilk, salt, pepper, baked 25 mins at 350F), cheddar cheese, and chorizo sausage gravy (olive oil, local chorizo, breakfast sausage, organic AP flour, paprika, granulated garlic, onion powder, half and half, whole milk, salt, pepper, fresh thyme). Pressed on a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: Well, I'm going to tell you the first thing, as scared of it as I was. For some reason, the way you've made it, the way you've seasoned it, it really is not overly heavy. It's delicious. The biscuit is light and flaky in there. The crunch of the hash brown inside is really nice. But the kicker for me is the gravy, bro. But I just have a bowl of gravy. This is a ski bum delight 101. Well done, man.

restaurants.name: OB Noodle House
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, ho dad, this is the joint. How's the food? Food's amazing. And the story is even better. That's my kind of joint. This is the OB Noodle House.

dishes.description: Mid-thigh chicken wings seasoned with garlic, salt, pepper, sugar, and marinated for a day in chili oil satay sauce (hot oil, ground ginger, garlic, brown lemongrass, ground onions, stirred for 40 mins, chili paste, chili flake, Hoppin' Arrow peppers, Thai chili pepper, peanut butter, hoisin sauce, simmered for an hour, then rested for a day). Wings are fried at 350F, then wok-tossed with hot oil, garlic, pepper, seasoning (sugar, granulated garlic, white pepper), and green onions. Garnished with cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my gosh. Yeah. A little bit of heat, not too much. Good amount of spice to it. A little bit of sweet. Just a little bit of the crispiness on the skin. They're tender. They're juicy. There's a lot of flavor, a lot of depth of flavor. I'm impressed.

restaurants.name: Joey's Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Lahaina in a strip mall, here to check out a joint. It's Filipino-Asian fusion. I didn't see that one coming. This is Joey's Kitchen.

dishes.description: Pig's feet boiled for 4-5 hours with garlic, onions, palm vinegar, baking powder, salt, peppercorn, and fish sauce to tenderize and remove impurities. Strained, dried in oven at 200F for 30 mins, then deep-fried until crispy. Served with a Filipino dipping sauce (soy sauce, spice vinegar with turmeric/chilies, fish sauce, rice wine vinegar, garlic, Hawaiian chilies, green onions, Hawaiian sea salt) and kimchi (salted cabbage, garlic and onion puree, kimchi chili powder, fish sauce, vinegar, fermented 3-4 days). Garnished with Tim Tim chilies (oil, garlic, shallots, ginger, shishito peppers, water, vinegar, fish sauce, black pepper, sea salt, cooked until liquid evaporates).
dishes.guy_response: The shishito is dynamite. That crunch right there. That gigantic chicharron. See, that's the key, drying it out in the oven. There's tons of flavor connected to all those bones. It's so tender, and it's so moist. The dipping sauce I need by the gallon, though. That's a game changer. And the kimchi refreshing that palate, that's the winner bite right there.

restaurants.name: Dolce and Clemente's
restaurants.guy_intro: You know, I love being from California. We got some of the best of the best. But what I wish we had more of is old school Italian delis like this, where they're scratch making their own desserts. They got a deli case full of everything. We're talking about prepared meals. We're talking fresh pasta, homemade sausage. I mean, you name it, they got it. So the next time you find yourself in Robbinsville, New Jersey, you got to check out Dolce and Clemente's.

dishes.description: House-made high-gluten pizza dough (water, yeast, salt, sugar, oil, mixed 10-12 mins, rested overnight, folded and kneaded, rolled) filled with prosciutto, ham, mortadella, super salsa (grandfather's recipe), pepperoni, provolone cheese, and crushed black pepper. Baked at 350F for 38 minutes. Served with extra virgin olive oil and locatelli cheese, garnished with parsley.
dishes.guy_response: Ridiculous. When you cook these Italian meats and you let all the fat cook into the dough, you just get all the flavor. The depth of flavor in this is massive. The key, though, is how you cook the meat on the outside of the bread. Gives it that crunch. Out of bounds. Seriously, out of bounds.

restaurants.name: Pinky's West Side Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're on the west side of Charlotte, North Carolina. Now, you remember the Penguin. Everybody remembers the Penguin. Well, it closed, but the guy, Greg, opened up a new joint over here on the west side. And you know what? This is Pinky's West Side Grill.

dishes.description: Three 5oz burger patties (pound of meat) with three pieces of American cheese, house southern slaw, mustard, chopped onions, and house-made chili (80-20 ground beef, water, yellow onion, fresh garlic, chili powder, black pepper, seasoning salt, coriander, hot sauce, cumin, tomato paste, cooked down).
dishes.guy_response: Greg, I'm telling you, it's fantastic. You're attacking this thing. It's like a coyote got a hold of it. It's a sloppy piece of equipment, but it's delicious and filling.

dishes.description: 21-25 count shrimp dipped in Dipsy Dog corn dog mix batter (water, whisked until creamy), then deep-fried until golden and crispy.
dishes.guy_response: They're so good. It tastes like a corn dog. Sweet, crunchy batter. Straight from the fair. Straight from the fair. Not salty.

restaurants.name: Fox Brothers Barbecue
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Atlanta, Georgia. And I am looking for what? Exactly. Some real deal barbecue. You know what it is, guy? With this Crate 502 in here, we just got to bump the timing up a little bit. That's all it is. So you call your buddy, Rutledge, who knows a lot about cars and a ton about barbecue. This is the real deal? This is the real deal. This is Fox Brothers Barbecue.

dishes.description: A chopped beef sandwich, not a traditional burger. Made with certified Angus beef brisket (trimmed, seasoned with salt and pepper, smoked overnight for 10-12 hours at 200-205F), chopped up. Served on a toasted bun with house-made jalapeno mayo, pimento cheese patty (cheddar, pepper jack, mayonnaise, pimentos, cayenne pepper, mixed by hand), peppered bacon, pickles, tomato, and onion.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's a great burger. You're just like cleaning up over there like a lion that just ate a gazelle. It's super tender brisket. When you chop that brisket up, you get a chance to incorporate that fat, so it makes it even a little bit more unctuous. And adding the pimento cheese and adding the veggies on top of it brings that burger element to it. That's Fox Brothers right there. Damn, man. Come on. That's full speed, man. That's all throttle, no bottle, man. That is just getting it done.

restaurants.name: Hardina
restaurants.guy_intro: I've heard about this funky little Indonesian joint that is run by two daughters and their mom. This is Hardina. It's just wonderful having international food like Indonesian food here in Philadelphia.

dishes.description: Country chicken boiled for 2 hours with ginger paste (candlenut, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, turmeric, turmeric powder), bruised lemongrass, Indonesian bay leaf, turmeric, salt. Coconut milk added and cooked for 20 mins. Chicken is strained, dried, and then fried with the skimmed ginger paste. Served with rice and sambal terasi (Thai chilies, shrimp paste, shallots, tomatoes, lime leaf, red peppers, sugar, salt, paprika, blended and cooked).
dishes.guy_response: I love the yard bird. This little ginger fry crunch that's on the outside, I keep chasing it around because I want more of it. The sambal is the bomb. With the rice, with the crunch, with the fry, with the chicken, when you can get that optimum bite, it was dynamite.

restaurants.name: Pingala
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Burlington, Vermont, to check out a joint. Where this guy's getting such acclaim, people are saying that he should be the driver of the vegan bust of Flavortown. This is Pingala.

dishes.description: Flour tortilla filled with oat cheese (rolled oats, nutritional yeast, turmeric, garlic powder, salt, lemon juice, oil, tapioca flour, boiled), brown rice, chili (sunflower seed oil, fresh garlic, mirepoix, serranos, chili powder, cumin, paprika, cinnamon, salt, brown sugar, tomatoes, organic tomato paste, tamari, lentils, black beans, cooked 1-1.5 hrs), cashew cheese (soaked cashews, sweet potato puree, nutritional yeast, cayenne, oregano, garlic powder, turmeric, salt, pepper, lemon juice, hot water), crunchy tortillas, not avocado mash (chickpeas, peas, nutritional yeast, lemon extract, herb oil - fresh garlic, raw onion, rosemary, sage, tarragon, parsley, salt, oil), tomato, scallion, cilantro, and pickled jalapeno. Folded and pressed on a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: It's outstanding. The textures. The seasoning. The fake avocado. Yeah. Probably was the foundation of me getting my mind blown. I mean, out of vegetarian, vegan dishes I've had in my life, this is easily in the top five. If not top three. I would challenge anybody who doesn't think they like vegetarian vegan to come down here and try this. It's remarkable. Get on a plane and come to Vermont, remarkable.

restaurants.name: Pyrenees Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: You know on Triple D, I love finding the old school joints. And boy, do I have one for you. A joint from 1901. So here I am on the east side of Bakersfield, California, right by the Southern Pacific Railroad. Now from 1901 to 1935, this place was a lot of different joints. But since 1935, everybody in Bakersfield knows this place. This is the Pyrenees Cafe.

dishes.description: Chicken seasoned with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, chicken base, fresh garlic, and olive oil, marinated for at least 2 hours. Then dipped in buttermilk and dredged in seasoned flour (black pepper, salt). Pan-fried (Basque way, not deep-fried) for 15-20 minutes. Served with green beans and french fries, and a garlic sauce (fresh garlic, olive oil, butter, parsley).
dishes.guy_response: But the garlic and the flavor inside the chicken is what is really nice. It's tender. It's juicy. Lots of flavor. The crust on the chicken is great. I have restaurants called Johnny Garlic's. I have one in Bakersfield. But if I was going to ever make fried garlic chicken, that would be the example. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: Paradise Valley Burger Company
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Phoenix, Arizona, right by Paradise Valley to check out a joint where this guy is doing some crazy things to burgers. Let's see. He's putting eggs on burgers. He's cooking burgers in booths. He's even deep frying burgers. This is Paradise Valley Burger Company.

dishes.description: Two 2oz burger patties (4oz total) deep-fried after being sprayed with heavy cream and dredged in flour. Served on a square-cut toasted brioche bun (cut square for full contact) with lettuce, country fried gravy (olive oil, diced red onion, sliced jalapenos, sliced garlic, salt, pepper, heavy cream, fresh thyme, simmered 15-20 mins, blended), fried onions, and tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: I just listened to you talk about how you make this burger. Cutting the bun so you can get full contact. Making the patties thin so you can put a nice crust. It's legit. It's enthusiastic. It takes the regular, everyday burger to another level. I mean, it really is, man. It's great.

dishes.description: Burger patties beer-battered (amber beer, salt, pepper, flour) and deep-fried for 2.5 minutes. Topped with green chili bacon vodka cream sauce (olive oil, rendered bacon, sliced red onion, jalapenos, garlic, vodka, heavy cream, salt, pepper, roasted green chilies - seeded, stemmed, skinned, cilantro, blended) and pickled Fresno chilies (sugar, liquid smoke, whiskey, pickled for 2 days).
dishes.guy_response: Hands down, what makes that burger was Fresno chilies. I agree, man. You got all kinds of great crunch. Salt. A little spice coming in there. You've got a passion for it. And it comes out of the burger. You're about to make a grown man cry. I'm telling you what. This is prime time, buddy.

restaurants.name: Chaps
restaurants.guy_intro: On Triple D, when we hit an historic place like Baltimore, well, we got to use the inside track of a buddy who lives here. So regardless of the weather or how busy this dude is, we're at Charm City Cakes to talk to the cake bad boy, Duff Goldman, and find out one of his favorite joints. I think probably Chaps. Chaps. Let's get some pit beef.

dishes.description: Bottom round beef cooked on a charcoal pit grill at 400-500F, pulled at desired doneness (medium rare to well done), thinly sliced, and piled high on a roll. Served with white onions, salt, pepper, and horseradish.
dishes.guy_response: You know, I like the little char to it. But I gotta say, the medley of the white onion is the best. The really nice, bright horseradish, a little bit of salt and pepper, a great roll. Not too much bread. I hate when you get too much bread and it starts to become... It's all about the meat. Wow. Mm. That was all horseradish. Good for you. Whoa. There it is. My toes are tingling. I mean, seriously. I go like, wow. Welcome to Baltimore. It's spicy. It's moist. The meat is so tender. I give it way past a 10.

dishes.dish_name: The Bulldog (Pit Beef and Sausage Combo)
dishes.description: A combo sandwich with pit beef and sausage on a roll, with cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I didn't think the pit beef would get any better. Got a little sausage kicker. Mm.

dishes.dish_name: Custom Combo (Pit Beef, Ham, Corned Beef)
dishes.description: A large sandwich with pit beef, ham, and corned beef on a roll.
dishes.guy_response: Is there anything wrong with this picture? Actually, no. There's not. When the bread won't even wrap around, that is so much flavor. Sweetness of the ham, that briny, pickly spice on the corned beef, and then just a nice little bit of the sauce. It's just so good. It's so good. It's so good. It's so good. It's so good. And then just a nice, smoky flavor from the pit beef. The roll that can withstand the pressure. This is like sandwich engineering right here. Good mouthful. It is. Good mouthful.

dishes.description: A massive custom-built sandwich for Duff Goldman, starting with pit beef, jalapenos, horseradish, ham, a burger patty (cut in half), nacho cheese sauce, sausage, pickles, chopped beef, and turkey.

restaurants.name: Reno's Place
restaurants.guy_intro: Now, you watch Triple D, you know I love Italian food. It's got to be handmade. It's got to be homemade. It's got to be the real deal. And from what the locals tell me, Reno's place is all that.

dishes.description: House-made pasta (flour, eggs, salt, extra virgin olive oil, mixed by hand, sheeted with pasta machine) filled with a mixture of fresh ricotta (from Italy), Parmigiano-Reggiano, parsley, salt, pepper, and chilled lobster meat (boiled lobsters, shocked in ice bath, meat removed from tail and legs, chopped, sauteed with olive oil, shallots, fresh parsley, salt, pepper, cooled). Raviolis are made with a mold, sealed, frozen, then boiled for 2.5 minutes. Served with a sauce made from extra virgin olive oil, shallots, lobster meat, salt, pepper, brandy, and grated Romitano-ritano cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I can feel the firmness of the pasta. Mmm. That's ridiculous. I don't know if I should hug you or kidnap you to California.

restaurants.name: Grammy's Goodies
restaurants.guy_intro: I heard about this family that started off in the fair business, and then they went and opened a restaurant. This is Grammy's Goodies.

dishes.description: Layers of noodles, grandmother's sundae sauce (olive oil, pork ribs, prosciutto, garlic salt, onion, parsley, fresh tomatoes, fresh basil, black pepper, salt, tomato paste, water, cooked 3 hours), ricotta cheese mixture (whole milk, ricotta cheese, black pepper, eggs, fresh parsley, mozzarella provolone blend, romano cheese), Nana's meatballs (half pork, half ground beef, chopped onions, soft bread crumbs, basil, garlic, parsley, romano cheese, salt, black pepper, eggs, pan-fried then baked 15 mins at 350F), and sausage (family recipe, 100 years old). Baked for about 3 hours.
dishes.guy_response: So I know that this is the lasagna portion for the entire family. Lots of ricotta, delicious meatballs, tender pasta, dynamite sauce. This is old school, American Italian, exactly the kind of lasagna I would expect to go and get at Grammy's house. Dynamite.

restaurants.name: Florence's Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: It's been about 12 years since I've had this place since we first came to OKC. I can't figure out how I missed a joint that's been doing it for 67 years. They've been doing this country cooking, and they're getting known now for something called yam fried chicken. Oh, that's right up my alley. This is Florence's Restaurant.

dishes.description: Spare ribs or St. Louis-style ribs cut, brined in lemon juice and vinegar water for a day or longer. Seasoned with salt and pepper, then dredged in seasoned flour (salt, corn starch, baking powder, pepper). Fried for 15 minutes. Served with house-made barbecue sauce (water, sugar, onion powder, barbecue spice, cumin, mustard, liquid smoke, vinegar, ketchup, cooked down).
dishes.guy_response: That's tasty. Not too much batter. Nice and juicy. I think the key was that you soaked them in the vinegar. Got a nice little flavor from that. Oh, there's good tang in that sauce, ma'am. Thank you. I'm gonna have more fried ribs.

dishes.description: Chicken seasoned with salt and pepper, then dredged in seasoned flour. Fried in Dutch ovens. Served with candy yams (cooked yams, sugar, butter, cooked until syrupy) and collard greens (cooked with water, baking soda, sugar, salt, chicken stock for 1.5 hours).
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. You really seasoned your chicken well. There's a lot of flavor in that pot. I love collard greens. Nice and tender, not mushy. Perfectly cooked chicken, tender candied yams, and you. Package deal. Fantastic. I need that yam fried chicken. Amazing. Tender and juicy on the inside. The yam sauce just made it so good. Made it so delicious.

restaurants.name: Pizzeria Lola
restaurants.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola.

dishes.description: Pizza with a 48-hour fermented dough (poolish made with water, yeast, flour, mixed 5-6 mins, sat 17 hours; then mixed with Italian flour, more yeast, cold water, Italian sea salt, mixed 10 mins, proofed 10 mins, cut into 9oz balls, cooled overnight). Topped with house red sauce (fresh ground California tomatoes - Stanislaus, red-peared tomatoes, tomato puree, salt, dried basil, dried oregano, granulated garlic, onion powder), Wisconsin ricotta, house-made fennel sausage (ground Berkshire pork, toasted fennel seed, fresh chopped garlic, chili flakes, salt, fresh ground pepper, massaged, flattened, baked at 400F for 15-20 mins), and natural-casing pepperoni. Cooked in a wood-fired oven at 550-600F.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in it and the real flavor, and the fennel's rocking this. A lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your average pizza. Everyday pizza joint on the corner, sausage and pepperoni. You gotta try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni's supposed to taste like.
